system.description: Literally “Thunder”, the El Trueno style is based on using a whip with brutal efficiency. It is said that this style was perfected by the torturers of the Inquisition, who could not only use a whip to strip a man’s hide from his bones, but also beat him senseless with the handle or strangle him.

benefit.description: Practitioners of the El Trueno Style learn how to kill with a whip. A whip does 1d3 Dmg instead of 1d3-1. When using a whip as a garrote to Choke, you can still take a Reaction or Yield Advantage, but doing so forces you to release your grip.
benefit.name: Deadly Weapon

finalSecret.description: With a flick of your wrist, you can cause the whip to loop around your enemy’s neck and cut off their breath. On a Mighty Success with a Bladework attack, you can make a free Choke/Crush attack as if your opponent were Grappled. If the attack succeeds, you begin strangling them immediately and they are also Grappled. If the Choke/Crush fails, the whip uncoils and the opponent is not Grappled.
finalSecret.name: Hangman’s Jig
